Meaning.En: A social gathering of friends, family, or colleagues for celebration or entertainment.
Meaning.Example: We’re hosting a small party for her birthday next Saturday.

Situation.Vi: Một buổi tụ họp vui vẻ với âm nhạc, đồ ăn và bạn bè.
Situation.En: A joyful gathering with music, food, and friends.

Situation.Vi: Người tham gia thường mặc trang phục phù hợp chủ đề (như tiệc hóa trang).
Situation.En: Guests often wear costumes or attire matching the party theme (e.g., costume party).

Meaning.En: A political organization that seeks to influence government policy and elect candidates to office.
Meaning.Example: The two major political parties in the U.S. are the Democratic and Republican Parties.

Situation.Vi: Các đảng phái tổ chức đại hội để đề ra chương trình hành động.
Situation.En: Parties hold conventions to outline their platforms and policies.

Meaning.En: A person or group involved in a dispute, negotiation, or legal proceeding.
Meaning.Example: Both parties agreed to settle the case out of court.

Situation.Vi: Trong hợp đồng, “party A” và “party B” chỉ các bên ký kết.
Situation.En: In contracts, “Party A” and “Party B” refer to the signatories.

Meaning.Vi: tham gia tiệc, đi dự tiệc
Meaning.En: To attend or participate in a social gathering or celebration.
Meaning.Example: She decided to party with her friends instead of studying.

Situation.Vi: Sau một tuần làm việc căng thẳng, họ muốn party để thư giãn.
Situation.En: After a stressful week of work, they want to party and relax.

Meaning.Vi: ăn uống, tiệc tùng quá mức (thường mang tính tiêu cực)
Meaning.En: To engage in excessive eating, drinking, or revelry—often used informally or critically.
Meaning.Example: They partied all night and woke up with a terrible hangover.

Situation.Vi: Sinh viên thường party vào cuối tuần trước kỳ thi.
Situation.En: Students often party on weekends before exams—though not always wisely.
